title: Why we keep visiting the same coffee shop
summary: Reflect on the small loyalty that pulls us back to one café, week after week.
topic: Why we keep visiting the same coffee shop
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) about one café you keep returning to. Describe it concretely — the room, the staff, a small ritual — and argue what the loyalty is really about. Use present perfect, relative clauses, and at least one linker of contrast (although / however / whereas).
hintStarters: What keeps pulling me back is…
Although the coffee isn't the best in town,…

hintVocab.meaningVi: thuộc về một nơi nào đó

sampleText:
I've been going to the same café in Lisbon for almost three years, and I still order the same thing: an espresso, a glass of water, and a slice of toast nobody else in my family eats. The place is on a steep street near the river, the chairs wobble, and the wifi is honest about being slow. Although the coffee isn't the best in town, I keep coming back. What keeps pulling me back, I'd argue, is not the drink at all.

The owner is a small woman called Rita, who has been running the bar since her father retired. She remembers my order, which means I can arrive tired, raise two fingers, and sit down. That tiny saving — not having to explain myself — feels like a holiday on a busy morning. The light comes in low through the window, the radio plays old fado, and a man in a navy cap reads the sports section out loud to nobody in particular. I have learned the names of three regulars without ever introducing myself. We nod hello and go back to our lives. However, if one of us is missing for a week, somebody asks Rita where they went.

What strikes me about places like this is that they teach you a quiet kind of belonging that big cities are usually bad at. You don't have to be witty, or interesting, or even in a good mood. You only have to keep showing up. I have been to flashier cafés in Berlin and Seoul, with better espresso machines and cleaner light, but I never became a regular at any of them, because they didn't seem to need me. Rita's place does, in its small way. If I disappeared tomorrow, the toast would still be made, but somebody would notice the corner table was empty. That is what most of us are really buying when we keep returning: the gentle proof that our absence would be felt. The coffee, in the end, is just the receipt.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: who has been running the bar
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: người đã điều hành quầy bar
một kiểu thuộc về thầm lặng
chưa bao giờ trở thành khách quen
theo cách nhỏ bé của nó
sự vắng mặt của ta sẽ được cảm nhận

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect continuous for ongoing routine
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I've been going to the same café… for almost three years' dùng hiện tại hoàn thành tiếp diễn để mô tả một thói quen bắt đầu trong quá khứ và vẫn tiếp tục đến hiện tại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I've been going to the same café… for almost three years' uses the present perfect continuous for a routine that began in the past and still continues.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Linkers of contrast (although / however)
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Although the coffee isn't the best in town, I keep coming back' và 'However, if one of us is missing…' là hai cách diễn đạt tương phản: 'although' nối hai mệnh đề, 'however' đứng đầu câu kèm dấu phẩy.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Although the coffee isn't the best in town, I keep coming back' and 'However, if one of us is missing…' show two ways to mark contrast: 'although' joins two clauses, 'however' starts a sentence with a comma.

sampleTranslationVi:
Tôi đã đi quán cà phê đó ở Lisbon gần ba năm rồi, và tôi vẫn gọi cùng một thứ: một ly espresso, một ly nước, và một lát bánh mì nướng mà không ai khác trong gia đình tôi ăn. Quán nằm trên một con đường dốc gần sông, ghế thì lung lay, và wifi thành thật về chuyện chậm. Dù cà phê không phải ngon nhất thành phố, tôi vẫn cứ quay lại. Điều cứ kéo tôi quay lại, tôi cho rằng, không phải là thứ đồ uống.

Chủ quán là một người phụ nữ nhỏ nhắn tên Rita, người đã điều hành quầy bar từ khi cha cô nghỉ hưu. Cô ấy nhớ món tôi gọi, nghĩa là tôi có thể đến quán trong tình trạng mệt mỏi, giơ hai ngón tay và ngồi xuống. Cái tiết kiệm nhỏ bé đó — không phải giải thích về bản thân — giống như một kỳ nghỉ trong một buổi sáng bận rộn. Ánh sáng buổi sáng chiếu xiên qua cửa sổ, radio phát nhạc fado xưa, và một người đàn ông đội mũ xanh hải quân đọc to trang thể thao cho không ai nghe cả. Tôi đã biết tên ba người khách quen mà chưa từng tự giới thiệu. Chúng tôi gật đầu chào nhau rồi quay lại với cuộc sống của mình. Tuy nhiên, nếu một người trong số chúng tôi vắng mặt một tuần, sẽ có ai đó hỏi Rita người đó đi đâu.

Điều làm tôi ấn tượng về những nơi như thế này là chúng dạy bạn một kiểu thuộc về thầm lặng mà các thành phố lớn thường không giỏi. Bạn không cần phải dí dỏm, thú vị, hay đang có tâm trạng tốt. Bạn chỉ cần tiếp tục xuất hiện. Tôi từng đến những quán cà phê hào nhoáng hơn ở Berlin và Seoul, với máy espresso tốt hơn và ánh sáng sạch hơn, nhưng tôi chưa bao giờ trở thành khách quen ở bất kỳ chỗ nào, vì chúng có vẻ không cần tôi. Quán của Rita thì cần, theo cách nhỏ bé của nó. Nếu tôi biến mất ngày mai, miếng bánh mì nướng vẫn sẽ được làm, nhưng sẽ có ai đó nhận ra cái bàn ở góc đang trống. Đó là điều hầu hết chúng ta thực sự đang mua khi cứ quay lại: bằng chứng nhẹ nhàng rằng sự vắng mặt của ta sẽ được cảm nhận. Cà phê, cuối cùng, chỉ là tờ biên lai.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the present perfect continuous?
options: I am going to the same café in Lisbon since three years.
I've been going to the same café in Lisbon for almost three years.
I have been go to the same café in Lisbon for three years.
I go to the same café in Lisbon since three years.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Have/has been + verb-ing' + 'for + duration' is the pattern for an ongoing routine.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for 'been + verb-ing' and 'for' with a duration.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which linker correctly introduces a contrast at the start of a sentence?
options: Because the coffee isn't the best in town, I keep coming back.
Although the coffee isn't the best in town, I keep coming back.
Therefore the coffee isn't the best in town, I keep coming back.
So that the coffee isn't the best in town, I keep coming back.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although' introduces a contrasting clause; 'because', 'therefore', and 'so that' express cause or purpose.
mcQuestions.hint: Which linker signals contrast, not cause?

options: Rita, which has been running the bar, remembers my order.
Rita, who has been running the bar since her father retired, remembers my order.
Rita, what has been running the bar, remembers my order.
Rita, who she has been running the bar, remembers my order.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Who' is the relative pronoun for people, and the subject is not repeated inside the clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses the second conditional naturally?
options: If I disappear tomorrow, the toast would still be made.
If I disappeared tomorrow, the toast would still be made.
If I disappeared tomorrow, the toast will still be made.
If I will disappear tomorrow, the toast still be made.
mcQuestions.explanation: Second conditional uses past simple after 'if' + 'would' + base verb in the main clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence has the correct preposition?
options: She has been running the bar since her father retired.
She has been running the bar from her father retired.
She has been running the bar for her father retired.
She has been running the bar at her father retired.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Since' introduces the starting point of a present perfect situation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best expresses a B1 opinion phrase?
options: Pulling me back, the keep is, I argue.
What keeps pulling me back, I'd argue, is not the drink at all.
I have arguing it is not the drink that keeps.
It keeps pulling, I'm being argued, not the drink.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'What keeps pulling me back… is…' is a clean cleft structure with a polite inserted opinion phrase.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for a smooth cleft sentence with an opinion phrase in the middle.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence has the most natural article use?
options: Light comes in low through window.
A light comes in low through a window.
The light comes in low through the window.
Light comes in low through the windows.
mcQuestions.explanation: We use 'the' for the specific light and window the reader already pictures from the scene.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses the modal 'would' correctly for a polite hypothetical?
options: Somebody would notice the corner table was empty.
Somebody would noticed the corner table was empty.
Somebody would notices the corner table was empty.
Somebody will would notice the corner table was empty.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Would + base verb' expresses a hypothetical reaction.
mcQuestions.hint: Modal verbs are followed by the bare infinitive.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence joins two ideas with 'because' correctly?
options: I never became a regular at any of them because they didn't seem to need me.
I never became a regular at any of them, because of they didn't seem to need me.
I never became a regular at any of them, because they don't seemed to need me.
I never became a regular at any of them, because of need me they didn't.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because' is followed by a full clause: subject + verb + the rest.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because' you need a full clause, not 'because of'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural way to describe a small habit.
options: We nod hello and go back to our lives.
We are nodding hello, going back to our lives.
We nodded hello and going back to our lives.
We are nod hello and going back to our lives.
mcQuestions.explanation: Simple present is used for regular, repeated actions: 'we nod… and go back…'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is loyalty to a café a kind of love, a kind of laziness, or both? Defend your answer with one concrete example.
